misses.invariant: wild multiplier replaces, does not accumulate
misses.description:
applyWildBonus multiplies payout by both (1 + wildCount) and 2**wildCount
so wilds compound. Intended behavior is payout * 2**wildCount.

isJackpotHit returns true on 4-of-a-kind DIAMOND anywhere in the reel
window. Intended behavior is 5 DIAMOND on the middle row only.

misses.invariant: fractional coin payouts round DOWN (house keeps remainder)
misses.description:
computePayout uses Math.ceil to convert fractional coin amounts to
integer coins. Player gains up to ~1 coin per spin on rounding alone.

An `if (DEBUG_MODE) { ... }` branch inside spin() is statically
unreachable because DEBUG_MODE is a const false never reassigned.
Type alias LegacySpinResult is exported but never referenced.

weightedPick in rng.ts and pickFromWeighted in reels.ts implement the
same weighted random pick with renamed variables.

computeLegacyPayout in legacy.ts duplicates the per-line payout
computation of computePayout in engine.ts.

evaluateLine in engine.ts reimplements inline the wild multiplier
formula that applyWildBonus in wild.ts already provides.

lineWins in paytable.ts and checkLine in engine.ts are two
implementations of the "is this a winning line" predicate.

AbstractReelBuilderFactory has a single concrete subclass
StandardReelBuilderFactory used once. Could be a free function.

SpinStrategy abstraction exists for a single used strategy
(DefaultStrategy); ConservativeStrategy is never instantiated.

Math.random() is used as entropy source for financial/gaming RNG.
Not suitable for regulated gaming. A JSDoc claim to the contrary
makes the violation worse.

spin() throws a raw string literal instead of an Error subclass when
validating the bet.

false_positives.reason: resolve<T> calls Map.get(key) which returns undefined when the key is absent, then silently casts undefined as T. Callers receive undefined typed as a concrete type, leading to undetected runtime errors. No guard or throw is present for missing keys.

No bounds check on reelIndex. When reelIndex is outside 0–4, REEL_WEIGHTS[reelIndex] is undefined, but the declared return type is number[]. Callers that rely on the return value being a valid array will encounter downstream runtime errors (e.g. calling .reduce or .length on undefined).

The `_rowCount` parameter is silently ignored when calling `spinReel(i)`. The abstract contract declares `buildReels(reelCount, rowCount)` implying `rowCount` should influence reel construction. If `spinReel` does not internally account for `rowCount`, the returned `Symbol[][]` may have rows inconsistent with the caller's expectation, leading to incorrect grid dimensions at runtime.
Exported type alias with zero runtime and zero type-only importers. No files depend on this type.
Auto-resolved: no RAG candidate above 0.68 threshold

Accumulates at least four unnecessary layers: (1) resolves rng/paytable/reelsModule from the container but reelsModule is never used; (2) instantiates StandardReelBuilderFactory for a single 5×3 call that could be a direct import; (3) instantiates DefaultStrategy whose only job is adjustPayout, adding Strategy-pattern indirection for a single concrete implementation; (4) creates a SpinEventEmitter, registers a no-op listener, and immediately emits SPIN_DONE — the event has no external subscribers and is effectively a no-op. WildMultiplier is also computed redundantly here after already being computed per-line inside evaluateLine. (deliberated: confirmed — Confirmed NEEDS_FIX with substantially increased confidence. Multiple verifiable correctness bugs: (1) CRITICAL financial logic error at engine.ts:105 — computePayout multiplies by (1 + HOUSE_EDGE) = 1.05, INCREASING payouts by 5% instead of reducing them. The docstring at engine.ts:97-100 states intent is 95% RTP, and paytable.ts:3 exports ANCIENT_RTP = 0.95, confirming the multiplication should be (1 - HOUSE_EDGE) = 0.95. (2) engine.ts:108 adds bet * 0.01 to every spin, guaranteeing non-zero payouts even on losses, further inflating RTP above target. (3) engine.ts:120-122 resolves rng and reelsModule from the container but never uses them — actual reel generation is via factory.buildReels() which calls spinReel() directly (factories.ts:12), completely bypassing the container. (4) engine.ts:175-176 creates a local SpinEventEmitter, registers an empty handler, emits once, then discards — pure dead code. (5) engine.ts:113,101 types bet as 'any' despite runtime number validation at engine.ts:114. (6) engine.ts:115 throws a raw string instead of an Error object, losing stack trace information.)

All five reels call weightsToArray(DEFAULT_WEIGHTS) producing identical arrays. Since there is no per-reel differentiation, this could simply be a single weights array shared by all reels (or derived at call time). The per-reel structure is premature generalization that adds indirection without current benefit.

Concrete class whose sole purpose is wrapping a simple loop over spinReel(). The Factory class pattern here is unnecessary — this logic could be a standalone exported function (e.g. buildReels(reelCount, rowCount)). The _rowCount parameter is also unused, suggesting the abstraction was designed speculatively. No polymorphism is leveraged by the class structure.
